title: baccharis dracunculifolia subsp. tandilensis
intro: Baccharis dracunculifolia subsp. tandilensis, also known as a type of shrub or small tree native to South America, is a plant species belonging to the Asteraceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in Argentina, for its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. Its medicinal properties and health benefits include the treatment of digestive issues, fever reduction, and wound healing, as well as providing relief from respiratory problems such as bronchitis and coughs. Baccharis dracunculifolia subsp. tandilensis can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or decoction, which can be used to take advantage of its various health benefits.
